# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.224.0/http/server.ts";

type Json =
  | null
  | boolean
  | number
  | string
  | Json[]
  | { [k: string]: Json };

type UserRecord = {
  userId: string;
  createdAt: number;
  updatedAt: number;
  data: Record<string, Json>;
  indexed: Record<string, string>;
};

type IndexValue = {
  userId: string;
  field: string;
  value: string;
  createdAt: number;
};

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

const corsHeaders = {
  "access-control-allow-origin": "*",
  "access-control-allow-methods": "GET,POST,PUT,DELETE,OPTIONS",
  "access-control-allow-headers": "content-type,authorization",
};

function json(data: unknown, init: ResponseInit = {}) {
  const headers = new Headers(init.headers);
  headers.set("content-type", "application/json; charset=utf-8");
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(corsHeaders)) headers.set(k, v);
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), { ...init, headers });
}

function badRequest(message: string, details?: unknown) {
  return json({ error: "bad_request", message, details }, { status: 400 });
}

function notFound(message: string) {
  return json({ error: "not_found", message }, { status: 404 });
}

function methodNotAllowed() {
  return json({ error: "method_not_allowed" }, { status: 405 });
}

function normalizeFieldName(field: string): string {
  const f = field.trim();
  if (!f) throw new Error("Empty field name");
  if (!/^[A-Za-z0-9_.-]+$/.test(f)) throw new Error(`Invalid field name: ${field}`);
  return f;
}

function normalizeIndexValue(value: string): string {
  const v = value.trim();
  if (!v) throw new Error("Empty value");
  if (v.length > 512) throw new Error("Value too long");
  return v;
}

function parseUserId(s: string | null): string {
  if (!s) throw new Error("Missing userId");
  const v = s.trim();
  if (!v) throw new Error("Missing userId");
  if (!/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1,128}$/.test(v)) throw new Error("Invalid userId");
  return v;
}

function getPathParts(url: URL): string[] {
  return url.pathname.replace(/\/+$/, "").split("/").filter(Boolean);
}

function parseJsonBody(req: Request): Promise<any> {
  const ct = req.headers.get("content-type") ?? "";
  if (!ct.toLowerCase().includes("application/json")) {
    return Promise.reject(new Error("Expected application/json body"));
  }
  return req.json();
}

function pickSearchFilters(url: URL): Record<string, string> {
  const out: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [k, v] of url.searchParams.entries()) {
    if (k === "userId" || k === "limit" || k === "cursor" || k === "fields") continue;
    if (k.startsWith("_")) continue;
    out[normalizeFieldName(k)] = normalizeIndexValue(v);
  }
  return out;
}

function parseLimit(url: URL): number {
  const raw = url.searchParams.get("limit");
  if (!raw) return 50;
  const n = Number(raw);
  if (!Number.isFinite(n) || n <= 0) throw new Error("Invalid limit");
  return Math.min(500, Math.floor(n));
}

function decodeCursor(raw: string | null): string | undefined {
  if (!raw) return undefined;
  try {
    return atob(raw);
  } catch {
    throw new Error("Invalid cursor");
  }
}

function encodeCursor(raw: string | undefined): string | null {
  if (!raw) return null;
  return btoa(raw);
}

function stableStringify(v: unknown): string {
  if (v === null || typeof v !== "object") return JSON.stringify(v);
  if (Array.isArray(v)) return JSON.stringify(v.map(stableStringify));
  const o = v as Record<string, unknown>;
  const keys = Object.keys(o).sort();
  const parts = keys.map((k) => `${JSON.stringify(k)}:${stableStringify(o[k])}`);
  return `{${parts.join(",")}}`;
}

function makeUserKey(userId: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", userId];
}

function makeUserIndexKey(userId: string, field: string, value: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", userId, field, value];
}

function makeUserFieldIndexKey(userId: string, field: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", userId, field];
}

async function getUser(userId: string): Promise<UserRecord | null> {
  const r = await kv.get<UserRecord>(makeUserKey(userId));
  return r.value ?? null;
}

async function putUser(
  userId: string,
  data: Record<string, Json>,
  indexed: Record<string, string>,
): Promise<UserRecord> {
  const now = Date.now();
  const existing = await getUser(userId);
  const next: UserRecord = {
    userId,
    createdAt: existing?.createdAt ?? now,
    updatedAt: now,
    data,
    indexed,
  };

  const atomic = kv.atomic();

  atomic.set(makeUserKey(userId), next);

  const prevIndexed = existing?.indexed ?? {};
  for (const [field, prevVal] of Object.entries(prevIndexed)) {
    const nextVal = indexed[field];
    if (nextVal === prevVal) continue;
    atomic.delete(makeUserIndexKey(userId, field, prevVal));
  }

  for (const [field, val] of Object.entries(indexed)) {
    const key = makeUserIndexKey(userId, field, val);
    const idx: IndexValue = { userId, field, value: val, createdAt: next.createdAt };
    atomic.set(key, idx);
  }

  const res = await atomic.commit();
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error("KV atomic commit failed");
  return next;
}

async function deleteUser(userId: string): Promise<boolean> {
  const existing = await getUser(userId);
  if (!existing) return false;

  const atomic = kv.atomic();
  atomic.delete(makeUserKey(userId));
  for (const [field, val] of Object.entries(existing.indexed ?? {})) {
    atomic.delete(makeUserIndexKey(userId, field, val));
  }
  const res = await atomic.commit();
  if (!res.ok) throw new Error("KV atomic commit failed");
  return true;
}

async function listAllUsers(
  limit: number,
  cursor?: string,
): Promise<{ items: UserRecord[]; cursor?: string }> {
  const iter = kv.list<UserRecord>({ prefix: ["users"] }, { limit, cursor });
  const items: UserRecord[] = [];
  for await (const entry of iter) {
    if (entry.key.length !== 2) continue;
    if (!entry.value) continue;
    items.push(entry.value);
  }
  return { items, cursor: iter.cursor ?? undefined };
}

async function searchUsersByFilters(
  userId?: string,
  filters: Record<string, string> = {},
  limit = 50,
  cursor?: string,
): Promise<{ items: UserRecord[]; cursor?: string }> {
  const filterEntries = Object.entries(filters);
  if (filterEntries.length === 0) {
    if (userId) {
      const u = await getUser(userId);
      return { items: u ? [u] : [], cursor: undefined };
    }
    return await listAllUsers(limit, cursor);
  }

  // Choose the most selective filter by iterating exactly on one precise index:
  // prefix: ["users", userId?, field, value] (exact) or ["users", <any userId>, field, value] not possible directly
  // For cross-user search, we maintain only per-user indexes; so without userId, we must scan users and filter in-memory.
  if (!userId) {
    // Fallback scan: list users then apply filters. Still uses KV efficiently for user listing.
    const { items: users, cursor: nextCursor } = await listAllUsers(limit, cursor);
    const matched = users.filter((u) => {
      for (const [f, v] of filterEntries) {
        if ((u.indexed?.[f] ?? "") !== v) return false;
      }
      return true;
    });
    return { items: matched, cursor: nextCursor };
  }

  // With userId we can do efficient lookups by using exact keys.
  // If multiple filters are provided, intersect by verifying user record against all filters (single user anyway).
  // For single user, best is direct user fetch + check.
  const u = await getUser(userId);
  if (!u) return { items: [], cursor: undefined };
  for (const [f, v] of filterEntries) {
    if ((u.indexed?.[f] ?? "") !== v) return { items: [], cursor: undefined };
    // Also demonstrate direct index existence check for this exact key.
    const idxKey = makeUserIndexKey(userId, f, v);
    const idx = await kv.get<IndexValue>(idxKey);
    if (!idx.value) return { items: [], cursor: undefined };
  }
  return { items: [u], cursor: undefined };
}

function parseIndexedFromBody(body: any): Record<string, string> {
  const indexed: Record<string, string> = {};
  if (body?.indexed && typeof body.indexed === "object" && !Array.isArray(body.indexed)) {
    for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(body.indexed as Record<string, unknown>)) {
      if (typeof v !== "string") continue;
      indexed[normalizeFieldName(k)] = normalizeIndexValue(v);
    }
  }
  return indexed;
}

function parseDataFromBody(body: any): Record<string, Json> {
  if (body?.data && typeof body.data === "object" && !Array.isArray(body.data)) {
    return body.data as Record<string, Json>;
  }
  if (body && typeof body === "object" && !Array.isArray(body)) {
    const { data, indexed, ...rest } = body as Record<string, unknown>;
    const candidate = (data && typeof data === "object" && !Array.isArray(data)) ? data : rest;
    return candidate as Record<string, Json>;
  }
  return {};
}

async function handleUsers(req: Request, url: URL): Promise<Response> {
  const parts = getPathParts(url);
  // Routes:
  // GET    /users?userId=...&field=value...
  // PUT    /users/:userId
  // GET    /users/:userId
  // DELETE /users/:userId
  // POST   /users/:userId (alias to PUT)
  if (parts.length === 1 && parts[0] === "users") {
    if (req.method !== "GET") return methodNotAllowed();

    let userId: string | undefined;
    try {
      const raw = url.searchParams.get("userId");
      if (raw) userId = parseUserId(raw);
    } catch (e) {
      return badRequest((e as Error).message);
    }

    let filters: Record<string, string>;
    let limit: number;
    let cur: string | undefined;
    try {
      filters = pickSearchFilters(url);
      limit = parseLimit(url);
      cur = decodeCursor(url.searchParams.get("cursor"));
    } catch (e) {
      return badRequest((e as Error).message);
    }

    const res = await searchUsersByFilters(userId, filters, limit, cur);
    return json({
      items: res.items,
      cursor: encodeCursor(res.cursor),
      appliedFilters: filters,
      userId: userId ?? null,
    });
  }

  if (parts.length === 2 && parts[0] === "users") {
    let userId: string;
    try {
      userId = parseUserId(parts[1]);
    } catch (e) {
      return badRequest((e as Error).message);
    }

    if (req.method === "GET") {
      const u = await getUser(userId);
      if (!u) return notFound("User not found");
      return json(u);
    }

    if (req.method === "DELETE") {
      const ok = await deleteUser(userId);
      if (!ok) return notFound("User not found");
      return json({ ok: true });
    }

    if (req.method === "PUT" || req.method === "POST") {
      let body: any;
      try {
        body = await parseJsonBody(req);
      } catch (e) {
        return badRequest((e as Error).message);
      }

      let indexed: Record<string, string>;
      let data: Record<string, Json>;
      try {
        indexed = parseIndexedFromBody(body);
        data = parseDataFromBody(body);
      } catch (e) {
        return badRequest((e as Error).message);
      }

      const u = await putUser(userId, data, indexed);
      return json(u, { status: 200 });
    }

    return methodNotAllowed();
  }

  return notFound("Route not found");
}

async function handleIndexProbe(req: Request, url: URL): Promise<Response> {
  // GET /probe?userId=...&field=...&value=...
  if (req.method !== "GET") return methodNotAllowed();
  let userId: string, field: string, value: string;
  try {
    userId = parseUserId(url.searchParams.get("userId"));
    field = normalizeFieldName(url.searchParams.get("field") ?? "");
    value = normalizeIndexValue(url.searchParams.get("value") ?? "");
  } catch (e) {
    return badRequest((e as Error).message);
  }

  const key = makeUserIndexKey(userId, field, value); // ['users', userId, filterParam...]
  const r = await kv.get<IndexValue>(key);
  return json({ key, exists: !!r.value, value: r.value ?? null });
}

async function handleDebug(req: Request, url: URL): Promise<Response> {
  // GET /debug/keys?userId=...&field=...
  if (req.method !== "GET") return methodNotAllowed();
  const parts = getPathParts(url);
  if (parts.join("/") !== "debug/keys") return notFound("Route not found");

  let userId: string;
  try {
    userId = parseUserId(url.searchParams.get("userId"));
  } catch (e) {
    return badRequest((e as Error).message);
  }

  const fieldRaw = url.searchParams.get("field");
  const limitRaw = url.searchParams.get("limit");
  const limit = Math.min(200, Math.max(1, Number(limitRaw ?? "50") || 50));

  const prefix: Deno.KvKey = fieldRaw
    ? makeUserFieldIndexKey(userId, normalizeFieldName(fieldRaw))
    : ["users", userId];

  const iter = kv.list<unknown>({ prefix }, { limit });
  const keys: Deno.KvKey[] = [];
  for await (const e of iter) keys.push(e.key);

  return json({ prefix, keys });
}

serve(async (req) => {
  if (req.method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders });
  }

  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const parts = getPathParts(url);

  if (parts.length === 0) {
    return json({
      name: "edge-kv-api",
      version: "1.0.0",
      routes: [
        "GET    /users?userId=...&<field>=<value>&limit=...&cursor=...",
        "GET    /users/:userId",
        "PUT    /users/:userId   { data: {...}, indexed: { role: 'admin', region: 'us' } }",
        "POST   /users/:userId  (alias to PUT)",
        "DELETE /users/:userId",
        "GET    /probe?userId=...&field=...&value=...",
        "GET    /debug/keys?userId=...&field=...&limit=...",
      ],
      keyScheme: [
        "User record: ['users', userId]",
        "Index:       ['users', userId, field, value]",
      ],
    });
  }

  if (parts[0] === "users") return await handleUsers(req, url);
  if (parts[0] === "probe") return await handleIndexProbe(req, url);
  if (parts[0] === "debug") return await handleDebug(req, url);

  return notFound("Route not found");
});
